description: End-to-end query → render → publish chain for partners with one human approval gate between the test cycle and the prod cycle. The evaluate phase runs all three stages against the test environment autonomously; FABER pauses before the release phase; after approval, all three stages run again against the prod environment. Re-querying for prod (rather than promoting test artifacts) ensures the prod run sees current upstream data — treat the test cycle as a structural sign-off, not a content lock-in. Each step wraps the corresponding corthography-press-* skill, so the run is governed by the same partner authorization scope as a direct CLI call.

evaluate.description: Test cycle (autonomous): query → render → publish, all against the test environment. After this completes, FABER pauses for human approval before the release phase.

steps.name: Query (test)
steps.description: Stage 1 of the test cycle: collect Corthodex API data for {asset} into the test data area.
steps.prompt: Use the corthography-press-query skill to start a template-query run for target {asset} with --env test --wait. Report the run_id and final status. If the run fails, stop the workflow — do not proceed to render.

steps.name: Render (test)
steps.description: Stage 2 of the test cycle: render Markdown for {asset} from the just-staged test data.
steps.prompt: Use the corthography-press-render skill to start a template-render run for target {asset} with --env test --wait. Report the run_id and final status.

steps.name: Publish (test)
steps.description: Stage 3 of the test cycle: publish the rendered Markdown to the test destination.
steps.prompt: Use the corthography-press-publish skill to start a template-publish run for target {asset} with --env test --wait. Report the run_id and final status. After this completes, summarize all three test-cycle run_ids and instruct the partner to inspect the test destination before approving the release phase.

release.description: Prod cycle (after human approval at the FABER gate): query → render → publish, all against the prod environment. Re-queries against prod rather than promoting test artifacts so the prod run reflects current upstream data.

steps.name: Query (prod)
steps.description: Stage 1 of the prod cycle: collect Corthodex API data for {asset} into the prod data area.
steps.prompt: Use the corthography-press-query skill to start a template-query run for target {asset} with --env prod --wait. Report the run_id and final status. If the run fails, stop the workflow — do not proceed to render.

steps.name: Render (prod)
steps.description: Stage 2 of the prod cycle: render Markdown for {asset} from the just-staged prod data.
steps.prompt: Use the corthography-press-render skill to start a template-render run for target {asset} with --env prod --wait. Report the run_id and final status.

steps.name: Publish (prod)
steps.description: Stage 3 of the prod cycle: publish the rendered Markdown to the prod destination. The API will pause this run at its own release gate (server-enforced); the CLI exits with code 2 when paused.
steps.prompt: Use the corthography-press-publish skill to start a template-publish run for target {asset} with --env prod --wait. After the CLI exits, report: (1) run_id, (2) exit code, (3) final status. Exit code 2 means the run is paused at the API's release gate — do NOT retry; instruct the partner to run /corthography-press-approve {run_id}, noting that auto_approve_prod=false on the partner registry means a press-core engineer must approve. After approval (or if auto_approve_prod=true and the run completed), summarize all three prod-cycle run_ids.

autonomy.description: Test cycle runs autonomously. Pauses before the prod cycle (release phase) for human approval. The publish-prod step also pauses at the API's own release gate (server-enforced) — this is by design and not bypassed by the FABER approval.
